14_1_1.NKJV: Now Solomon the son of David was strengthened in his kingdom, and the LORD his God was with him and exalted him exceedingly.
14_1_1.KJV: And Solomon the son of David was strengthened in his kingdom, and the LORD his God was with him, and magnified him exceedingly.
14_1_1.ESV: Solomon the son of David established himself in his kingdom, and the LORD his God was with him and made him exceedingly great.
14_1_1.NASB: Now Solomon the son of David established himself securely over his kingdom, and the LORD his God [was] with him and exalted him greatly.
14_1_1.NLT: Solomon son of David took firm control of his kingdom, for the LORD his God was with him and made him very powerful.
14_1_1.WEB: Solomon the son of David was firmly established in his kingdom, and Yahweh his God was with him, and made him exceedingly great.
14_1_1.CSB17: Solomon son of David strengthened his hold on his kingdom. The LORD his God was with him and highly exalted him.
14_1_1.AMP: Solomon the son of David established himself securely over his kingdom, and the LORD his God was with him and made him exceedingly great.
14_1_1.MSG: Solomon son of David took a firm grip on the reins of his kingdom. God was with him and gave him much help.
14_1_1.NIV: Solomon Asks for WisdomSolomon son of David established himself firmly over his kingdom, for the Lord his God was with him and made him exceedingly great.
14_1_1.NRSVCE: Solomon son of David established himself in his kingdom; the LORD his God was with him and made him exceedingly great.

14_1_2.NKJV: And Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the captains of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to every leader in all Israel, the heads of the fathers’ houses.
14_1_2.KJV: Then Solomon spake unto all Israel, to the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and to the judges, and to every governor in all Israel, the chief of the fathers.
14_1_2.ESV: Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to all the leaders in all Israel, the heads of fathers' houses.
14_1_2.NASB: Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the commanders of thousands and of hundreds and to the judges and to every leader in all Israel, the heads of the fathers' [households].
14_1_2.NLT: Solomon called together all the leaders of Israel — the generals and captains of the army, the judges, and all the political and clan leaders.
14_1_2.WEB: Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the captains of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to every prince in all Israel, the heads of the fathers’ households.
14_1_2.CSB17: Then Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to every leader in all Israel — the family heads.
14_1_2.AMP: Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the commanders of thousands and of hundreds and to the judges and to every leader in all Israel, the heads of the fathers' (ancestors') households.
14_1_2.MSG: Solomon addressed all Israel—the commanders and captains, the judges, every leader, and all the heads of families.
14_1_2.NIV: Then Solomon spoke to all Israel—to the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, to the judges and to all the leaders in Israel, the heads of families—
14_1_2.NRSVCE: Solomon summoned all Israel, the commanders of the thousands and of the hundreds, the judges, and all the leaders of all Israel, the heads of families.

14_1_3.NKJV: Then Solomon, and all the assembly with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon; for the tabernacle of meeting with God was there, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness.
14_1_3.KJV: So Solomon, and all the congregation with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon; for there was the tabernacle of the congregation of God, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness.
14_1_3.ESV: And Solomon, and all the assembly with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon, for the tent of meeting of God, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness, was there.
14_1_3.NASB: Then Solomon and all the assembly with him went to the high place which was at Gibeon, for God's tent of meeting was there, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness.
14_1_3.NLT: Then he led the entire assembly to the place of worship in Gibeon, for God’s Tabernacle was located there. (This was the Tabernacle that Moses, the LORD’s servant, had made in the wilderness.)
14_1_3.WEB: So Solomon, and all the assembly with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon; for God’s Tent of Meeting was there, which Yahweh’s servant Moses had made in the wilderness.
14_1_3.CSB17: Solomon and the whole assembly with him went to the high place that was in Gibeon because God’s tent of meeting, which the LORD’s servant Moses had made in the wilderness, was there.
14_1_3.AMP: Then Solomon and all the assembly went to the high place at Gibeon [to offer sacrifices, where the Canaanites had habitually worshiped], for God's Tent of Meeting was there, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness.
14_1_3.MSG: Then Solomon and the entire company went to the worship center at Gibeon—that's where the Tent of Meeting of God was, the one that Moses the servant of God had made in the wilderness.
14_1_3.NIV: and Solomon and the whole assembly went to the high place at Gibeon, for God’s Tent of Meeting was there, which Moses the Lord’s servant had made in the desert.
14_1_3.NRSVCE: Then Solomon, and the whole assembly with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon; for God’s tent of meeting, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness, was there.

14_1_4.NKJV: But David had brought up the ark of God from Kirjath Jearim to the place David had prepared for it, for he had pitched a tent for it at Jerusalem.
14_1_4.KJV: But the ark of God had David brought up from Kirjathjearim to the place which David had prepared for it: for he had pitched a tent for it at Jerusalem.
14_1_4.ESV: (But David had brought up the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the place that David had prepared for it, for he had pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem.)
14_1_4.NASB: However, David had brought up the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the place he had prepared for it, for he had pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem.
14_1_4.NLT: David had already moved the Ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the tent he had prepared for it in Jerusalem.
14_1_4.WEB: But David had brought God’s ark up from Kiriath Jearim to the place that David had prepared for it; for he had pitched a tent for it at Jerusalem.
14_1_4.CSB17: Now David had brought the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the place he had set up for it, because he had pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem,
14_1_4.AMP: But David had brought up the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the place he had prepared for it, because he had pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem.
14_1_4.MSG: The Chest of God, though, was in Jerusalem—David had brought it up from Kiriath Jearim, prepared a special place for it, and pitched a tent for it.
14_1_4.NIV: Now David had brought up the ark of God from Kiriath Jearim to the place he had prepared for it, because he had pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem.
14_1_4.NRSVCE: (But David had brought the ark of God up from Kiriath-jearim to the place that David had prepared for it; for he had pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem.)

14_1_5.NKJV: Now the bronze altar that Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made, he put before the tabernacle of the LORD; Solomon and the assembly sought Him there.
14_1_5.KJV: Moreover the brasen altar, that Bezaleel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made, he put before the tabernacle of the LORD: and Solomon and the congregation sought unto it. he put: or, was there
14_1_5.ESV: Moreover, the bronze altar that Bezalel the son of Uri, son of Hur, had made, was there before the tabernacle of the LORD. And Solomon and the assembly sought it out.
14_1_5.NASB: Now the bronze altar, which Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made, was there before the tabernacle of the LORD, and Solomon and the assembly sought it out.
14_1_5.NLT: But the bronze altar made by Bezalel son of Uri and grandson of Hur was there at Gibeon in front of the Tabernacle of the LORD. So Solomon and the people gathered in front of it to consult the LORD.
14_1_5.WEB: Moreover the bronze altar that Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made was there before Yahweh’s tabernacle; and Solomon and the assembly were seeking counsel there.
14_1_5.CSB17: but he put the bronze altar, which Bezalel son of Uri, son of Hur, had made, in front of the LORD’s tabernacle. Solomon and the assembly inquired of him there.
14_1_5.AMP: Now the bronze altar, which Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made was there before the tabernacle of the LORD, and Solomon and the assembly sought it out.
14_1_5.MSG: But the Bronze Altar that Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made was in Gibeon, in its place before the Tabernacle of God; and that is where Solomon and the congregation gathered to pray.
14_1_5.NIV: But the bronze altar that Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made was in Gibeon in front of the tabernacle of the Lord; so Solomon and the assembly inquired of him there.
14_1_5.NRSVCE: Moreover the bronze altar that Bezalel son of Uri, son of Hur, had made, was there in front of the tabernacle of the LORD. And Solomon and the assembly inquired at it.

14_1_6.NKJV: And Solomon went up there to the bronze altar before the LORD, which was at the tabernacle of meeting, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.KJV: And Solomon went up thither to the brasen altar before the LORD, which was at the tabernacle of the congregation, and offered a thousand burnt offerings upon it.
14_1_6.ESV: And Solomon went up there to the bronze altar before the LORD, which was at the tent of meeting, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.NASB: Solomon went up there before the LORD to the bronze altar which [was] at the tent of meeting, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.NLT: There in front of the Tabernacle, Solomon went up to the bronze altar in the LORD’s presence and sacrificed 1,000 burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.WEB: Solomon went up there to the bronze altar before Yahweh, which was at the Tent of Meeting, and offered one thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.CSB17: Solomon offered sacrifices there in the LORD’s presence on the bronze altar at the tent of meeting; he offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.AMP: And Solomon went up to the bronze altar before the LORD at the Tent of Meeting, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.MSG: Solomon worshiped God at the Bronze Altar in front of the Tent of Meeting; he sacrificed a thousand Whole-Burnt-Offerings on it.
14_1_6.NIV: Solomon went up to the bronze altar before the Lord in the Tent of Meeting and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.
14_1_6.NRSVCE: Solomon went up there to the bronze altar before the LORD, which was at the tent of meeting, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.

14_1_7.NKJV: On that night God appeared to Solomon, and said to him, “Ask! What shall I give you?”
14_1_7.KJV: In that night did God appear unto Solomon, and said unto him, Ask what I shall give thee.
14_1_7.ESV: In that night God appeared to Solomon, and said to him, “Ask what I shall give you.”
14_1_7.NASB: In that night God appeared to Solomon and said to him, 'Ask what I shall give you.'
14_1_7.NLT: That night God appeared to Solomon and said, “What do you want? Ask, and I will give it to you!”
14_1_7.WEB: That night, God appeared to Solomon and said to him, “Ask for what you want me to give you.”
14_1_7.CSB17: That night God appeared to Solomon and said to him: “Ask. What should I give you?”
14_1_7.AMP: That night God appeared to Solomon and said to him, "Ask what I shall give to you."
14_1_7.MSG: That night God appeared to Solomon. God said, "What do you want from me? Ask."
14_1_7.NIV: That night God appeared to Solomon and said to him, “Ask for whatever you want me to give you.”
14_1_7.NRSVCE: That night God appeared to Solomon, and said to him, “Ask what I should give you.”

14_1_8.NKJV: And Solomon said to God: “You have shown great mercy to David my father, and have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.KJV: And Solomon said unto God, Thou hast shewed great mercy unto David my father, and hast made me to reign in his stead.
14_1_8.ESV: And Solomon said to God, “You have shown great and steadfast love to David my father, and have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.NASB: Solomon said to God, 'You have dealt with my father David with great lovingkindness, and have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.NLT: Solomon replied to God, “You showed great and faithful love to David, my father, and now you have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.WEB: Solomon said to God, “You have shown great loving kindness to David my father, and have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.CSB17: And Solomon said to God: “You have shown great and faithful love to my father David, and you have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.AMP: Then Solomon said to God, "You have shown great lovingkindness and mercy to my father David, and have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.MSG: Solomon answered, "You were extravagantly generous with David my father, and now you have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.NIV: Solomon answered God, “You have shown great kindness to David my father and have made me king in his place.
14_1_8.NRSVCE: Solomon said to God, “You have shown great and steadfast love to my father David, and have made me succeed him as king.

14_1_9.NKJV: Now, O LORD God, let Your promise to David my father be established, for You have made me king over a people like the dust of the earth in multitude.
14_1_9.KJV: Now, O LORD God, let thy promise unto David my father be established: for thou hast made me king over a people like the dust of the earth in multitude. like: Heb. much as the dust of the earth
14_1_9.ESV: O LORD God, let your word to David my father be now fulfilled, for you have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.
14_1_9.NASB: 'Now, O LORD God, Your promise to my father David is fulfilled, for You have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.
14_1_9.NLT: O LORD God, please continue to keep your promise to David my father, for you have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth!
14_1_9.WEB: Now, Yahweh God, let your promise to David my father be established; for you have made me king over a people like the dust of the earth in multitude.
14_1_9.CSB17: LORD God, let your promise to my father David now come true. For you have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.
14_1_9.AMP: Now, O LORD God, Your promise to my father David is fulfilled, for You have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.
14_1_9.MSG: Establish, God, the words you spoke to my father, for you've given me a staggering task, ruling this mob of people.
14_1_9.NIV: Now, Lord God, let your promise to my father David be confirmed, for you have made me king over a people who are as numerous as the dust of the earth.
14_1_9.NRSVCE: O LORD God, let your promise to my father David now be fulfilled, for you have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.

14_1_10.NKJV: Now give me wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out and come in before this people; for who can judge this great people of Yours?”
14_1_10.KJV: Give me now wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out and come in before this people: for who can judge this thy people, that is so great?
14_1_10.ESV: Give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people, for who can govern this people of yours, which is so great?”
14_1_10.NASB: 'Give me now wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out and come in before this people, for who can rule this great people of Yours?'
14_1_10.NLT: Give me the wisdom and knowledge to lead them properly, for who could possibly govern this great people of yours?”
14_1_10.WEB: Now give me wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out and come in before this people; for who can judge this great people of yours?”
14_1_10.CSB17: Now grant me wisdom and knowledge so that I may lead these people, for who can judge this great people of yours?”
14_1_10.AMP: Give me wisdom and knowledge, so that I may go out and come in [performing my duties] before this people, for [otherwise] who can rule and administer justice to this great people of Yours?"
14_1_10.MSG: Yes, give me wisdom and knowledge as I come and go among this people—for who on his own is capable of leading these, your glorious people?"
14_1_10.NIV: Give me wisdom and knowledge, that I may lead this people, for who is able to govern this great people of yours?”
14_1_10.NRSVCE: Give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people, for who can rule this great people of yours?”

14_1_11.NKJV: Then God said to Solomon: “Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked riches or wealth or honor or the life of your enemies, nor have you asked long life—but have asked wisdom and knowledge for yourself, that you may judge My people over whom I have made you king—
14_1_11.KJV: And God said to Solomon, Because this was in thine heart, and thou hast not asked riches, wealth, or honour, nor the life of thine enemies, neither yet hast asked long life; but hast asked wisdom and knowledge for thyself, that thou mayest judge my people, over whom I have made thee king:
14_1_11.ESV: God answered Solomon, “Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked for possessions, wealth, honor, or the life of those who hate you, and have not even asked for long life, but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may govern my people over whom I have made you king,
14_1_11.NASB: God said to Solomon, 'Because you had this in mind, and did not ask for riches, wealth or honor, or the life of those who hate you, nor have you even asked for long life, but you have asked for yourself wisdom and knowledge that you may rule My people over whom I have made you king,
14_1_11.NLT: God said to Solomon, “Because your greatest desire is to help your people, and you did not ask for wealth, riches, fame, or even the death of your enemies or a long life, but rather you asked for wisdom and knowledge to properly govern my people —
14_1_11.WEB: God said to Solomon, “Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked riches, wealth, honor, or the life of those who hate you, nor yet have you asked for long life; but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself, that you may judge my people, over whom I have made you king,
14_1_11.CSB17: God said to Solomon, “Since this was in your heart, and you have not requested riches, wealth, or glory, or for the life of those who hate you, and you have not even requested long life, but you have requested for yourself wisdom and knowledge that you may judge my people over whom I have made you king,
14_1_11.AMP: God replied to Solomon, "Because this was in your heart and you did not ask for riches, possessions or honor and personal glory, or the life of those who hate you, nor have you even asked for long life, but you have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself so that you may rule and administer justice to My people over whom I have made you king,
14_1_11.MSG: God answered Solomon, "This is what has come out of your heart: You didn't grasp for money, wealth, fame, and the doom of your enemies; you didn't even ask for a long life. You asked for wisdom and knowledge so you could govern well my people over whom I've made you king.
14_1_11.NIV: God said to Solomon, “Since this is your heart’s desire and you have not asked for wealth, riches or honor, nor for the death of your enemies, and since you have not asked for a long life but for wisdom and knowledge to govern my people over whom I have made you king,
14_1_11.NRSVCE: God answered Solomon, “Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked for possessions, wealth, honor, or the life of those who hate you, and have not even asked for long life, but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may rule my people over whom I have made you king,

14_1_12.NKJV: wisdom and knowledge are granted to you; and I will give you riches and wealth and honor, such as none of the kings have had who were before you, nor shall any after you have the like.”
14_1_12.KJV: Wisdom and knowledge is granted unto thee; and I will give thee riches, and wealth, and honour, such as none of the kings have had that have been before thee, neither shall there any after thee have the like.
14_1_12.ESV: wisdom and knowledge are granted to you. I will also give you riches, possessions, and honor, such as none of the kings had who were before you, and none after you shall have the like.”
14_1_12.NASB: wisdom and knowledge have been granted to you. And I will give you riches and wealth and honor, such as none of the kings who were before you has possessed nor those who will come after you.'
14_1_12.NLT: I will certainly give you the wisdom and knowledge you requested. But I will also give you wealth, riches, and fame such as no other king has had before you or will ever have in the future!”
14_1_12.WEB: therefore wisdom and knowledge is granted to you. I will give you riches, wealth, and honor, such as none of the kings have had who have been before you had, and none after you will have.”
14_1_12.CSB17: wisdom and knowledge are given to you. I will also give you riches, wealth, and glory, unlike what was given to the kings who were before you, or will be given to those after you.”
14_1_12.AMP: wisdom and knowledge have been granted you. I will also give you riches, possessions, and honor, such as none of the kings who were before you has possessed nor will those who will come after you."
14_1_12.MSG: Because of this, you get what you asked for—wisdom and knowledge. And I'm presenting you the rest as a bonus—money, wealth, and fame beyond anything the kings before or after you had or will have."
14_1_12.NIV: therefore wisdom and knowledge will be given you. And I will also give you wealth, riches and honor, such as no king who was before you ever had and none after you will have.”
14_1_12.NRSVCE: wisdom and knowledge are granted to you. I will also give you riches, possessions, and honor, such as none of the kings had who were before you, and none after you shall have the like.”

14_1_13.NKJV: So Solomon came to Jerusalem from the high place that was at Gibeon, from before the tabernacle of meeting, and reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.KJV: Then Solomon came from his journey to the high place that was at Gibeon to Jerusalem, from before the tabernacle of the congregation, and reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.ESV: So Solomon came from the high place at Gibeon, from before the tent of meeting, to Jerusalem. And he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.NASB: So Solomon went from the high place which was at Gibeon, from the tent of meeting, to Jerusalem, and he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.NLT: Then Solomon returned to Jerusalem from the Tabernacle at the place of worship in Gibeon, and he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.WEB: So Solomon came from the high place that was at Gibeon, from before the Tent of Meeting, to Jerusalem; and he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.CSB17: So Solomon went to Jerusalem from the high place that was in Gibeon in front of the tent of meeting, and he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.AMP: So Solomon went from the high place at Gibeon, from the Tent of Meeting, to Jerusalem. And he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.MSG: Then Solomon left the worship center at Gibeon and the Tent of Meeting and went to Jerusalem. He set to work as king of Israel.
14_1_13.NIV: Then Solomon went to Jerusalem from the high place at Gibeon, from before the Tent of Meeting. And he reigned over Israel.
14_1_13.NRSVCE: So Solomon came from the high place at Gibeon, from the tent of meeting, to Jerusalem. And he reigned over Israel.

14_1_14.NKJV: And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen; he had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen, whom he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.
14_1_14.KJV: And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen: and he had a thousand and four hundred chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen, which he placed in the chariot cities, and with the king at Jerusalem.
14_1_14.ESV: Solomon gathered together chariots and horsemen. He had 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horsemen, whom he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.
14_1_14.NASB: Solomon amassed chariots and horsemen. He had 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horsemen, and he stationed them in the chariot cities and with the king at Jerusalem.
14_1_14.NLT: Solomon built up a huge force of chariots and horses. He had 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horses. He stationed some of them in the chariot cities and some near him in Jerusalem.
14_1_14.WEB: Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen. He had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen that he placed in the chariot cities, and with the king at Jerusalem.
14_1_14.CSB17: Solomon accumulated 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horsemen, which he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.
14_1_14.AMP: Solomon acquired chariots and horsemen; he had 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horsemen, and he stationed them in the cities [suited for the use] of chariots and with the king at Jerusalem.
14_1_14.MSG: Solomon collected chariots and horses: 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horses! He stabled them in the special chariot-cities as well as in Jerusalem.
14_1_14.NIV: Solomon accumulated chariots and horses; he had fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand horses, which he kept in the chariot cities and also with him in Jerusalem.
14_1_14.NRSVCE: Solomon gathered together chariots and horses; he had fourteen hundred chariots and twelve thousand horses, which he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.

14_1_15.NKJV: Also the king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stones, and he made cedars as abundant as the sycamores which are in the lowland.
14_1_15.KJV: And the king made silver and gold at Jerusalem as plenteous as stones, and cedar trees made he as the sycomore trees that are in the vale for abundance. made: Heb. gave
14_1_15.ESV: And the king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stone, and he made cedar as plentiful as the sycamore of the Shephelah.
14_1_15.NASB: The king made silver and gold as plentiful in Jerusalem as stones, and he made cedars as plentiful as sycamores in the lowland.
14_1_15.NLT: The king made silver and gold as plentiful in Jerusalem as stone. And valuable cedar timber was as common as the sycamore-fig trees that grow in the foothills of Judah.
14_1_15.WEB: The king made silver and gold to be as common as stones in Jerusalem, and he made cedars to be as common as the sycamore trees that are in the lowland.
14_1_15.CSB17: The king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stones, and he made cedar as abundant as sycamore in the Judean foothills.
14_1_15.AMP: The king made silver and gold as plentiful and common in Jerusalem as stones, and he made cedar as plentiful as the sycamore-fig trees in the lowland.
14_1_15.MSG: The king made silver and gold as common as rocks, and cedar as common as the fig trees in the lowland hills.
14_1_15.NIV: The king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stones, and cedar as plentiful as sycamore-fig trees in the foothills.
14_1_15.NRSVCE: The king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stone, and he made cedar as plentiful as the sycamore of the Shephelah.

14_1_16.NKJV: And Solomon had horses imported from Egypt and Keveh; the king’s merchants bought them in Keveh at the current price.
14_1_16.KJV: And Solomon had horses brought out of Egypt, and linen yarn: the king's merchants received the linen yarn at a price. Solomon: Heb. the going forth of the horses which was Solomon's
14_1_16.ESV: And Solomon's import of horses was from Egypt and Kue, and the king's traders would buy them from Kue for a price.
14_1_16.NASB: Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and from Kue; the king's traders procured them from Kue for a price.
14_1_16.NLT: Solomon’s horses were imported from Egypt and from Cilicia ; the king’s traders acquired them from Cilicia at the standard price.
14_1_16.WEB: The horses which Solomon had were brought out of Egypt and from Kue. The king’s merchants purchased them from Kue.
14_1_16.CSB17: Solomon’s horses came from Egypt and Kue. The king’s traders would get them from Kue at the going price.
14_1_16.AMP: Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and from Kue. The king's merchants purchased them [in large numbers] from Kue at a price.
14_1_16.MSG: His horses were brought in from Egypt and Cilicia, specially acquired by the king's agents.
14_1_16.NIV: Solomon’s horses were imported from Egypt and from Kue—the royal merchants purchased them from Kue.
14_1_16.NRSVCE: Solomon’s horses were imported from Egypt and Kue; the king’s traders received them from Kue at the prevailing price.

14_1_17.NKJV: They also acquired and imported from Egypt a chariot for six hundred shekels of silver, and a horse for one hundred and fifty; thus, through their agents, they exported them to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Syria.
14_1_17.KJV: And they fetched up, and brought forth out of Egypt a chariot for six hundred shekels of silver, and an horse for an hundred and fifty: and so brought they out horses for all the kings of the Hittites, and for the kings of Syria, by their means. means: Heb. hand
14_1_17.ESV: They imported a chariot from Egypt for 600 shekels of silver, and a horse for 150. Likewise through them these were exported to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Syria.
14_1_17.NASB: They imported chariots from Egypt for 600 [shekels] of silver apiece and horses for 150 apiece, and by the same means they exported them to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram.
14_1_17.NLT: At that time chariots from Egypt could be purchased for 600 pieces of silver, and horses for 150 pieces of silver. They were then exported to the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram.
14_1_17.WEB: They brought up and brought out of Egypt a chariot for six hundred pieces of silver, and a horse for one hundred fifty. They also exported them to the Hittite kings and the Syrian kings.
14_1_17.CSB17: A chariot could be imported from Egypt for fifteen pounds of silver and a horse for nearly four pounds. In the same way, they exported them to all the kings of the Hittites and to the kings of Aram through their agents.
14_1_17.AMP: And they imported chariots from Egypt for 600 shekels of silver apiece, and horses for 150 apiece; and in the same way they exported horses to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of the Arameans (Syrians).
14_1_17.MSG: Chariots from Egypt went for fifteen pounds of silver and a horse for about three and three-quarters of a pound of silver. Solomon carried on a brisk horse-trading business with the Hittite and Aramean royal houses.
14_1_17.NIV: They imported a chariot from Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver, and a horse for a hundred and fifty. They also exported them to all the kings of the Hittites and of the Arameans.
14_1_17.NRSVCE: They imported from Egypt, and then exported, a chariot for six hundred shekels of silver, and a horse for one hundred fifty; so through them these were exported to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram.

14_2_1.NKJV: Then Solomon determined to build a temple for the name of the LORD, and a royal house for himself.
14_2_1.KJV: And Solomon determined to build an house for the name of the LORD, and an house for his kingdom.
14_2_1.ESV: Now Solomon purposed to build a temple for the name of the LORD, and a royal palace for himself.
14_2_1.NASB: Now Solomon decided to build a house for the name of the LORD and a royal palace for himself.
14_2_1.NLT: Solomon decided to build a Temple to honor the name of the LORD, and also a royal palace for himself.
14_2_1.WEB: Now Solomon decided to build a house for Yahweh’s name, and a house for his kingdom.
14_2_1.CSB17: Solomon decided to build a temple for the name of the LORD and a royal palace for himself,
14_2_1.AMP: Now Solomon decided to build a house (temple) for the Name of the LORD, and a royal palace for himself.
14_2_1.MSG: Solomon gave orders to begin construction on the house of worship in honor of God and a palace for himself.
14_2_1.NIV: Preparations for Building the TempleSolomon gave orders to build a temple for the Name of the Lord and a royal palace for himself.
14_2_1.NRSVCE: Solomon decided to build a temple for the name of the LORD, and a royal palace for himself.

14_2_2.NKJV: Solomon selected seventy thousand men to bear burdens, eighty thousand to quarry stone in the mountains, and three thousand six hundred to oversee them.
14_2_2.KJV: And Solomon told out threescore and ten thousand men to bear burdens, and fourscore thousand to hew in the mountain, and three thousand and six hundred to oversee them.
14_2_2.ESV: And Solomon assigned 70,000 men to bear burdens and 80,000 to quarry in the hill country, and 3,600 to oversee them.
14_2_2.NASB: So Solomon assigned 70,000 men to carry loads and 80,000 men to quarry [stone] in the mountains and 3,600 to supervise them.
14_2_2.NLT: He enlisted a force of 70,000 laborers, 80,000 men to quarry stone in the hill country, and 3,600 foremen.
14_2_2.WEB: Solomon counted out seventy thousand men to bear burdens, eighty thousand men who were stone cutters in the mountains, and three thousand six hundred to oversee them.
14_2_2.CSB17: so he assigned 70,000 men as porters, 80,000 men as stonecutters in the mountains, and 3,600 as supervisors over them.
14_2_2.AMP: So Solomon assigned 70,000 men to carry loads, 80,000 men to quarry stone in the mountains, and 3,600 to supervise them.
14_2_2.MSG: Solomon assigned 70,000 common laborers, 80,000 to work the quarries in the mountains, and 3,600 foremen to manage the workforce.
14_2_2.NIV: He conscripted seventy thousand men as carriers and eighty thousand as stonecutters in the hills and thirty-six hundred as foremen over them.
14_2_2.NRSVCE: Solomon conscripted seventy thousand laborers and eighty thousand stonecutters in the hill country, with three thousand six hundred to oversee them.

14_2_3.NKJV: Then Solomon sent to Hiram king of Tyre, saying: As you have dealt with David my father, and sent him cedars to build himself a house to dwell in, so deal with me.
14_2_3.KJV: And Solomon sent to Huram the king of Tyre, saying, As thou didst deal with David my father, and didst send him cedars to build him an house to dwell therein, even so deal with me. Huram: or, Hiram
14_2_3.ESV: And Solomon sent word to Hiram the king of Tyre: “As you dealt with David my father and sent him cedar to build himself a house to dwell in, so deal with me.
14_2_3.NASB: Then Solomon sent [word] to Huram the king of Tyre, saying, 'As you dealt with David my father and sent him cedars to build him a house to dwell in, so do for me.
14_2_3.NLT: Solomon also sent this message to King Hiram at Tyre: “Send me cedar logs as you did for my father, David, when he was building his palace.
14_2_3.WEB: Solomon sent to Huram the king of Tyre, saying, “As you dealt with David my father, and sent him cedars to build him a house in which to dwell, so deal with me.
14_2_3.CSB17: Then Solomon sent word to King Hiram of Tyre: Do for me what you did for my father David. You sent him cedars to build him a house to live in.
14_2_3.AMP: Then Solomon sent word to Hiram king of Tyre, saying, "As you dealt with my father David and sent him cedars to build himself a house in which to live, [please] do the same for me.
14_2_3.MSG: Then Solomon sent this message to King Hiram of Tyre: "Send me cedar logs, the same kind you sent David my father for building his palace.
14_2_3.NIV: Solomon sent this message to Hiram king of Tyre:“Send me cedar logs as you did for my father David when you sent him cedar to build a palace to live in.
14_2_3.NRSVCE: Solomon sent word to King Huram of Tyre: “Once you dealt with my father David and sent him cedar to build himself a house to live in.

14_2_4.NKJV: Behold, I am building a temple for the name of the LORD my God, to dedicate it to Him, to burn before Him sweet incense, for the continual showbread, for the burnt offerings morning and evening, on the Sabbaths, on the New Moons, and on the set feasts of the LORD our God. This is an ordinance forever to Israel.
14_2_4.KJV: Behold, I build an house to the name of the LORD my God, to dedicate it to him, and to burn before him sweet incense, and for the continual shewbread, and for the burnt offerings morning and evening, on the sabbaths, and on the new moons, and on the solemn feasts of the LORD our God. This is an ordinance for ever to Israel. sweet: Heb. incense of spices
14_2_4.ESV: Behold, I am about to build a house for the name of the LORD my God and dedicate it to him for the burning of incense of sweet spices before him, and for the regular arrangement of the showbread, and for burnt offerings morning and evening, on the Sabbaths and the new moons and the appointed feasts of the LORD our God, as ordained forever for Israel.
14_2_4.NASB: 'Behold, I am about to build a house for the name of the LORD my God, dedicating it to Him, to burn fragrant incense before Him and [to set out] the showbread continually, and to offer burnt offerings morning and evening, on sabbaths and on new moons and on the appointed feasts of the LORD our God, this [being required] forever in Israel.
14_2_4.NLT: I am about to build a Temple to honor the name of the LORD my God. It will be a place set apart to burn fragrant incense before him, to display the special sacrificial bread, and to sacrifice burnt offerings each morning and evening, on the Sabbaths, at new moon celebrations, and at the other appointed festivals of the LORD our God. He has commanded Israel to do these things forever.
14_2_4.WEB: Behold, I am about to build a house for the name of Yahweh my God, to dedicate it to him, to burn before him incense of sweet spices, for the continual show bread, and for the burnt offerings morning and evening, on the Sabbaths, on the new moons, and on the set feasts of Yahweh our God. This is an ordinance forever to Israel.
14_2_4.CSB17: Now I am building a temple for the name of the LORD my God in order to dedicate it to him for burning fragrant incense before him, for displaying the rows of the Bread of the Presence continuously, and for sacrificing burnt offerings for the morning and the evening, the Sabbaths and the New Moons, and the appointed festivals of the LORD our God. This is ordained for Israel permanently.
14_2_4.AMP: Observe, I am about to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God, dedicated to Him, to burn fragrant incense [of sweet spices] before Him, to set out the showbread continually, and to offer burnt offerings morning and evening, on Sabbaths, New Moons, and on the festivals of the LORD our God, as ordained forever in Israel.
14_2_4.MSG: I'm about to build a house of worship in honor of God, a holy place for burning perfumed incense, for setting out holy bread, for making Whole-Burnt-Offerings at morning and evening worship, and for Sabbath, New Moon, and Holy Day services of worship—the acts of worship required of Israel.
14_2_4.NIV: Now I am about to build a temple for the Name of the Lord my God and to dedicate it to him for burning fragrant incense before him, for setting out the consecrated bread regularly, and for making burnt offerings every morning and evening and on Sabbaths and New Moons and at the appointed feasts of the Lord our God. This is a lasting ordinance for Israel.
14_2_4.NRSVCE: I am now about to build a house for the name of the LORD my God and dedicate it to him for offering fragrant incense before him, and for the regular offering of the rows of bread, and for burnt offerings morning and evening, on the sabbaths and the new moons and the appointed festivals of the LORD our God, as ordained forever for Israel.

14_2_5.NKJV: And the temple which I build will be great, for our God is greater than all gods.
14_2_5.KJV: And the house which I build is great: for great is our God above all gods.
14_2_5.ESV: The house that I am to build will be great, for our God is greater than all gods.
14_2_5.NASB: 'The house which I am about to build [will be] great, for greater is our God than all the gods.
14_2_5.NLT: “This must be a magnificent Temple because our God is greater than all other gods.
14_2_5.WEB: “The house which I am building will be great; for our God is greater than all gods.
14_2_5.CSB17: The temple that I am building will be great, for our God is greater than any of the gods.
14_2_5.AMP: The house I am going to build will be great, for our God is greater than all the gods.
14_2_5.MSG: "The house I am building has to be the best, for our God is the best, far better than competing gods.
14_2_5.NIV: “The temple I am going to build will be great, because our God is greater than all other gods.
14_2_5.NRSVCE: The house that I am about to build will be great, for our God is greater than other gods.

14_2_6.NKJV: But who is able to build Him a temple, since heaven and the heaven of heavens cannot contain Him? Who am I then, that I should build Him a temple, except to burn sacrifice before Him?
14_2_6.KJV: But who is able to build him an house, seeing the heaven and heaven of heavens cannot contain him? who am I then, that I should build him an house, save only to burn sacrifice before him? is able: Heb. hath retained, or, obtained strength
14_2_6.ESV: But who is able to build him a house, since heaven, even highest heaven, cannot contain him? Who am I to build a house for him, except as a place to make offerings before him?
14_2_6.NASB: 'But who is able to build a house for Him, for the heavens and the highest heavens cannot contain Him? So who am I, that I should build a house for Him, except to burn [incense] before Him?
14_2_6.NLT: But who can really build him a worthy home? Not even the highest heavens can contain him! So who am I to consider building a Temple for him, except as a place to burn sacrifices to him?
14_2_6.WEB: But who is able to build him a house, since heaven and the heaven of heavens can’t contain him? Who am I then, that I should build him a house, except just to burn incense before him?
14_2_6.CSB17: But who is able to build a temple for him, since even heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain him? Who am I then that I should build a temple for him except as a place to burn incense before him?
14_2_6.AMP: But who is able to build a house for Him, since the heavens and [even] the highest heavens cannot contain Him? Who am I to build a house for Him, except [as a place] to burn incense before Him?
14_2_6.MSG: But who is capable of building such a structure? Why, the skies—the entire cosmos!—can't begin to contain him. And me, who am I to think I can build a house adequate for God—burning incense to him is about all I'm good for!
14_2_6.NIV: But who is able to build a temple for him, since the heavens, even the highest heavens, cannot contain him? Who then am I to build a temple for him, except as a place to burn sacrifices before him?
14_2_6.NRSVCE: But who is able to build him a house, since heaven, even highest heaven, cannot contain him? Who am I to build a house for him, except as a place to make offerings before him?

14_2_7.NKJV: Therefore send me at once a man skillful to work in gold and silver, in bronze and iron, in purple and crimson and blue, who has skill to engrave with the skillful men who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, whom David my father provided.
14_2_7.KJV: Send me now therefore a man cunning to work in gold, and in silver, and in brass, and in iron, and in purple, and crimson, and blue, and that can skill to grave with the cunning men that are with me in Judah and in Jerusalem, whom David my father did provide. to grave: Heb. to grave gravings
14_2_7.ESV: So now send me a man skilled to work in gold, silver, bronze, and iron, and in purple, crimson, and blue fabrics, trained also in engraving, to be with the skilled workers who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, whom David my father provided.
14_2_7.NASB: 'Now send me a skilled man to work in gold, silver, brass and iron, and in purple, crimson and violet [fabrics], and who knows how to make engravings, to [work] with the skilled men whom I have in Judah and Jerusalem, whom David my father provided.
14_2_7.NLT: “So send me a master craftsman who can work with gold, silver, bronze, and iron, as well as with purple, scarlet, and blue cloth. He must be a skilled engraver who can work with the craftsmen of Judah and Jerusalem who were selected by my father, David.
14_2_7.WEB: “Now therefore send me a man skillful to work in gold, in silver, in brass, in iron, and in purple, crimson, and blue, and who knows how to engrave engravings, to be with the skillful men who are with me in Judah and in Jerusalem, whom David my father provided.
14_2_7.CSB17: Therefore, send me an artisan who is skilled in engraving to work with gold, silver, bronze, and iron, and with purple, crimson, and blue yarn. He will work with the artisans who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, appointed by my father David.
14_2_7.AMP: Now send me a man who is skilled to work in gold, silver, bronze, and iron, and in purple, crimson, and blue fabrics, and who knows how to make engravings, to work with the skilled men who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, whom my father David provided.
14_2_7.MSG: I need your help: Send me a master artisan in gold, silver, bronze, iron, textiles of purple, crimson, and violet, and who knows the craft of engraving; he will supervise the trained craftsmen in Judah and Jerusalem that my father provided.
14_2_7.NIV: “Send me, therefore, a man skilled to work in gold and silver, bronze and iron, and in purple, crimson and blue yarn, and experienced in the art of engraving, to work in Judah and Jerusalem with my skilled craftsmen, whom my father David provided.
14_2_7.NRSVCE: So now send me an artisan skilled to work in gold, silver, bronze, and iron, and in purple, crimson, and blue fabrics, trained also in engraving, to join the skilled workers who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, whom my father David provided.

14_2_8.NRSVCE: Send me also cedar, cypress, and algum timber from Lebanon, for I know that your servants are skilled in cutting Lebanon timber. My servants will work with your servants

14_2_11.NKJV: Then Hiram king of Tyre answered in writing, which he sent to Solomon: Because the LORD loves His people, He has made you king over them.

14_2_12.NKJV: Hiram also said: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel, who made heaven and earth, for He has given King David a wise son, endowed with prudence and understanding, who will build a temple for the LORD and a royal house for himself!
14_2_12.KJV: Huram said moreover, Blessed be the LORD God of Israel, that made heaven and earth, who hath given to David the king a wise son, endued with prudence and understanding, that might build an house for the LORD, and an house for his kingdom. endued: Heb. knowing prudence and understanding
14_2_12.ESV: Hiram also said, “Blessed be the LORD God of Israel, who made heaven and earth, who has given King David a wise son, who has discretion and understanding, who will build a temple for the LORD and a royal palace for himself.
14_2_12.NASB: Then Huram continued, 'Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who has made heaven and earth, who has given King David a wise son, endowed with discretion and understanding, who will build a house for the LORD and a royal palace for himself.
